summary: @PeterDiamandis What you say is true, but nonetheless our AI will be great. Whether it is the best remains to be seen, but I will never give up. Never. Space(XAI) is only 3 years old. That’s half the age of Anthropic and quarter the age of OpenAI. Let’s see where things stand 3 years from

parent.text: Elon doesn't need to run the best AI model. He needs to control the best hardware. Nvidia is the most valuable company on Earth and their models aren't the most popular. The hyperscaler play works.
parent.name: Peter H. Diamandis, MD
parent.embed_html:
Elon doesn't need to run the best AI model. He needs to control the best hardware. Nvidia is the most valuable company on Earth and their models aren't the most popular. The hyperscaler play works.

— Peter H. Diamandis, MD (@PeterDiamandis) May 26, 2026

ai_summary: Peter Diamandis argues that controlling superior hardware infrastructure (like Nvidia's dominance) matters more than having the most popular AI model, while Elon counters that xAI will deliver great results despite being only 3 years old compared to older competitors. The insight reflects a critical business strategy debate: whether competitive advantage in AI comes from compute/hardware control versus model quality, with Elon positioning xAI's youth as an asset rather than liability in a multi-year race.

Let’s see where things stand 3 years from…

This demonstrates SpaceX's strategic approach to serving dual markets while maintaining organizational and regulatory boundaries between commercial and defense divisions.
